title: phebalium rude subsp. amblycarpum
intro: Phebalium rude subsp. amblycarpum, also known as the Coastal Baeckea, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its white or yellowish flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the Coastal Baeckea has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of the plant have been associated with relief from colds, coughs, and fever, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections. Coastal Baeckea is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its herbal benefits, and can be used externally as a wash or poultice for wound healing and skin irritations.
